hypothesis: Adding a '→ [agent]' routing suggestion to each actionable-now finding (e.g., '→ dev', '→ seo', '→ analyst', '→ boss-decision') eliminates boss's triage overhead and increases adoptions above 9/week. Evidence: Cycle 3 KEEP (9 adoptions) confirmed tier classification primes action; the remaining friction is boss deciding which agent receives each item. Pre-routing removes that decision step. Cycle 4 runs post-Reyco sprint in a cleaner operational tempo, removing the sprint-mode confound from Cycle 3.
learning: Measured 7 confirmed adoptions across Apr 29-May 6 14:41Z window vs target >9 (cycle 2 baseline). Ecosystem scan: 3 adoptions (boss cited claude-code-system-prompts in digest, ACK'd trends-agent-claude with full workflow plan, ACK'd Cloudflare autonomy signal with watch-flag + 2 triggers). Domain scan: 4 adoptions (SEO queued intent-saturation audit, content banked 4 items, ads revised CTR thresholds, prospector documented email length gap). Result falls short of cycle 2 baseline (9). Key confound: pre-routing changed measurement path — boss received fewer items to triage directly by design. However, specialist adoptions were not higher enough to compensate. Additional confound: Reyco domain cutover (May 2) reduced boss bandwidth mid-window. May 6 dev routings (4 items) arrived too close to window close for adoption within measurement period. Core finding: routing destination tags alone are insufficient — recipients get the item but lack a specific suggested action, leaving adoption conversion low. DISCARD.
changes_description: Updated ecosystem_scan_adoption surface to cycle 4 format: added explicit → [agent] routing tags to each actionable-now finding (→ dev, → seo, → analyst, → boss-decision). Boss receives fleet-level digest; specialists receive targeted send-message per domain finding. Channel post mirrors tiered classification for paper trail. Change from cycle 3: routing destination now pre-specified per item, eliminating boss triage overhead of deciding which agent receives each signal.
